preamble: The elision of the uvular nasal [ɴ] is a common historical development, especially in word-final positions where it may first nasalize a preceding vowel before disappearing completely.

examples.note: Historical uvular nasals in final positions often elide, potentially leaving nasalization on the preceding vowel.
The moraic nasal, which can be [ɴ] word-finally, sometimes undergoes elision or significant weakening in casual speech.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
